euler_problems: Problem 70 - Totient Permutation: Find n (1 < n < 10^7) where phi(n) is a permutation of n and n/phi(n) is minimised.
explanation: The included code defines a function to determine if two integers are permutations of each other based on their digit counts. In the main function, it calculates Euler's totient function values for all integers from 1 to 10 million, tracking the time taken for these calculations. It then checks for pairs of numbers, i and φ(i), that maintain a specific ratio and are permutations of each other, printing the results along with the time taken for this computation.
name: 2010-11-24_pe70
subject: PE70
summary: This program computes the Euler's totient function for numbers up to 10 million and identifies pairs of numbers that are permutations of each other and have a specific ratio.
